    47→import { MTag } from './renderers/tag'
    48→import { Video } from './renderers/video'
    49→import { getFootNoteDomId, getFootNoteRefDomId } from './utils/get-id'
    50→import { redHighlight } from './utils/redHighlight'
    51→
    52→export type MarkdownVariant = 'post' | 'note' | 'comment'
    53→
    54→export interface MdProps {
    55→  value?: string
    56→
    57→  style?: React.CSSProperties
    58→  readonly renderers?: Partial<MarkdownToJSX.PartialRules>
    59→  wrapperProps?: React.DetailedHTMLProps<
    60→    React.HTMLAttributes<HTMLDivElement>,
    61→    HTMLDivElement
    62→  >
    63→  wrapperRef?: React.Ref<HTMLElement>
    64→  codeBlockFully?: boolean
    65→  className?: string
    66→  as?: React.ElementType
    67→
    68→  allowsScript?: boolean
    69→
    70→  removeWrapper?: boolean
    71→
    72→  variant?: MarkdownVariant
    73→}
    74→
    75→const debugValue = isDev
    76→  ? ''
    77→  : //       '```component shadow with-styles\n' +
    78→    //         `import=https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/@innei/react-cdn-components@0.0.33/dist/components/ShadowDOMTest.js
    79→    // name=MDX.ShadowDOMTest
    80→    // height=4 05` +
    81→    //         '\n' +
    82→    //         '```',
    83→    //     ].join('')
    84→    null
    85→
    86→export const Markdown: FC<MdProps & MarkdownToJSX.Options & PropsWithChildren> =
    87→  memo((props) => {
    88→    const {
    89→      value,
    90→      renderers,
    91→      style,
    92→      wrapperProps = {},
    93→      wrapperRef,
    94→      codeBlockFully = false,
    95→      className,
    96→      overrides,
    97→      extendsRules,
    98→
    99→      as: As = 'div',
   100→      allowsScript = false,
   101→      removeWrapper = false,
   102→      variant = 'post',
   103→
   104→      ...rest
   105→    } = props
   106→
   107→    const MHeader = useMemo(() => createMarkdownHeadingComponent(), [])
   108→
   109→    const node = useMemo(() => {
   110→      const mdContent = debugValue || value || props.children
   111→
   112→      if (!mdContent) return null
   113→      if (typeof mdContent != 'string') return null
   114→
   115→      const mdElement = compiler(mdContent, {
   116→        slugify,
   117→        doNotProcessHtmlElements: ['tab', 'style', 'script'] as any[],
   118→        wrapper: null,
   119→
   120→        overrides: {
   121→          p: MParagraph,
   122→
   123→          thead: MTableHead,
   124→          tr: MTableRow,
   125→          tbody: MTableBody,
   126→          td: MTableTd,
   127→          th: MTableTh,
   128→          table: MTable,
   129→          // FIXME: footer tag in raw html will renders not as expected, but footer tag in this markdown lib will wrapper as linkReferer footnotes
   130→          footer: MFootNote,
   131→          details: MDetails,
   132→          img: MarkdownImage,
   133→          tag: MTag,
   134→
   135→          Tabs,
   136→
   137→          tab: Tab,
   138→          video: Video,
   139→
   140→          // for custom react component
   141→          // Tag: MTag,
   142→
   143→          LinkCard,
   144→          Gallery,
   145→          script: allowsScript ? Script : undefined!,
   146→
   147→          ...overrides,
   148→        },
   149→
   150→        overrideRules: {
   151→          [RuleType.heading]: {
   152→            render(node, output, state) {
   153→              return (
   154→                <MHeader id={node.id} level={node.level} key={state?.key}>
   155→                  {output(node.children, state!)}
   156→                </MHeader>
   157→              )
   158→            },
   159→          },
   160→          [RuleType.textMarked]: {
   161→            render(node, output, state) {
   162→              return (
   163→                <mark key={state?.key} className="rounded-md">
   164→                  <span className="px-1">{output(node.children, state!)}</span>
   165→                </mark>
   166→              )
   167→            },
   168→          },
   169→          [RuleType.gfmTask]: {
   170→            render(node, _, state) {
   171→              return (
   172→                <input
   173→                  type="checkbox"
   174→                  key={state?.key}
   175→                  checked={node.completed}
   176→                  readOnly
   177→                  className="size-[1em]!"
   178→                />
   179→              )
   180→            },
   181→          },
   182→
   183→          [RuleType.link]: {
   184→            render(node, output, state) {
   185→              const { target, title } = node
   186→
   187→              let realText = ''
   188→
   189→              for (const child of node.children) {
   190→                if (child.type === RuleType.text) {
   191→                  realText += child.text
   192→                }
   193→              }
   194→
   195→              return (
   196→                <MarkdownLink
   197→                  href={sanitizer(target)!}
   198→                  title={title}
   199→                  key={state?.key}
   200→                  text={realText}
   201→                >
   202→                  {output(node.children, state!)}
   203→                </MarkdownLink>
   204→              )
   205→            },
   206→          },
   207→
   208→          [RuleType.blockQuote]: {
   209→            render(node, output, state) {
   210→              return (
   211→                <MBlockQuote key={state?.key} alert={node.alert}>
   212→                  {output(node.children, state!)}
   213→                </MBlockQuote>
   214→              )
   215→            },
   216→          },
   217→
   218→          [RuleType.footnoteReference]: {
   219→            render(node, output, state) {
   220→              const { footnoteMap, text } = node
   221→              const footnote = footnoteMap.get(text)
   222→              const linkCardId = (() => {
   223→                try {
   224→                  const thisUrl = new URL(
   225→                    footnote?.footnote?.replace(': ', '') ?? '',
   226→                  )
   227→                  const isCurrentHost =
   228→                    thisUrl.hostname === window.location.hostname
   229→                  if (!isCurrentHost && !isDev) {
   230→                    return
   231→                  }
   232→                  const { pathname } = thisUrl
   233→                  return pathname.slice(1)
   234→                } catch {
   235→                  return
   236→                }
   237→              })()
   238→
   239→              return (
   240→                <Fragment key={state?.key}>
   241→                  <FloatPopover
   242→                    wrapperClassName="inline"
   243→                    as="span"
   244→                    triggerElement={
   245→                      <a
   246→                        href={`${getFootNoteDomId(text)}`}
   247→                        onClick={(e) => {
   248→                          e.preventDefault()
   249→                          const id = getFootNoteDomId(text)
   250→                          springScrollToElement(
   251→                            document.getElementById(id)!,
   252→                            -window.innerHeight / 2,
   253→                          )
   254→                          redHighlight(id)
   255→                        }}
   256→                      >
   257→                        <sup
   258→                          id={`${getFootNoteRefDomId(text)}`}
   259→                        >{`[^${text}]`}</sup>
   260→                      </a>
   261→                    }
   262→                    type="tooltip"
   263→                  >
   264→                    {footnote?.footnote?.slice(1)}
   265→                  </FloatPopover>
   266→                  {linkCardId && (
   267→                    <LinkCard
   268→                      id={linkCardId}
   269→                      source={LinkCardSource.MixSpace}
   270→                    />
   271→                  )}
   272→                </Fragment>
   273→              )
   274→            },
   275→          },
   276→
   277→          [RuleType.codeBlock]: {
   278→            render(node, output, state) {
   279→              return (
   280→                <CodeBlockRender
   281→                  key={state?.key}
   282→                  content={node.text}
   283→                  lang={node.lang}
   284→                  attrs={node?.rawAttrs}
   285→                />
   286→              )
   287→            },
   288→          },
   289→          [RuleType.codeInline]: {
   290→            render(node, output, state) {
   291→              return (
   292→                <code
   293→                  key={state?.key}
   294→                  className="rounded-md bg-zinc-200 px-2 font-mono dark:bg-neutral-800"
   295→                >
   296→                  {node.text}
   297→                </code>
   298→              )
   299→            },
   300→          },
   301→
   302→          [RuleType.orderedList]: listRule as any,
   303→          [RuleType.unorderedList]: listRule as any,
   304→
   305→          ...renderers,
   306→        },
   307→        extendsRules: {
   308→          spoilder: SpoilerRule,
   309→          mention: MentionRule,
   310→
   311→          ins: InsertRule,
   312→          kateX: KateXRule,
   313→          kateXBlock: KateXBlockRule,
   314→          container: ContainerRule,
   315→          ...extendsRules,
   316→        },
   317→        ...rest,
   318→      })
   319→
   320→      return mdElement
   321→    }, [
   322→      value,
   323→      props.children,
   324→      allowsScript,
   325→      overrides,
   326→      extendsRules,
   327→      renderers,
   328→      rest,
   329→      MHeader,
   330→    ])
   331→
   332→    if (removeWrapper) return <Suspense>{node}</Suspense>
   333→
   334→    return (
   335→      <ErrorBoundary>
   336→        <Suspense>
   337→          <As
   338→            style={style}
   339→            {...wrapperProps}
   340→            ref={wrapperRef as any}
   341→            className={clsx(
   342→              'markdown',
   343→              codeBlockFully ? 'markdown--code-fully' : undefined,
   344→              variant !== 'post' && `markdown--${variant}`,
   345→              className,
   346→            )}
   347→          >
   348→            {node}
   349→          </As>
   350→        </Suspense>
   351→      </ErrorBoundary>
   352→    )
   353→  })
   354→Markdown.displayName = 'Markdown'
   355→
   356→export const MainMarkdown: FC<
   357→  MdProps & MarkdownToJSX.Options & PropsWithChildren
   358→> = (props) => {
   359→  const { wrapperProps = noopObj } = props
   360→  const setMainMarkdownRef = useCallback((element: HTMLElement | null) => {
   361→    setMainMarkdownElement(element)
   362→  }, [])
   363→
   364→  return (
   365→    <Markdown
   366→      as="main"
   367→      {...props}
   368→      wrapperRef={setMainMarkdownRef}
   369→      wrapperProps={useMemo(
   370→        () => ({
   371→          ...wrapperProps,
   372→          id: MAIN_CONTENT_ID,
   373→        }),
   374→        [wrapperProps],
   375→      )}
   376→    />
   377→  )
   378→}
   379→
   380→// not complete, but probably good enough
   381→function slugify(str: string) {
   382→  return (
   383→    str
   384→      .replaceAll(/[ÀÁÂÃÄÅæ]/gi, 'a')
   385→      .replaceAll(/ç/gi, 'c')
   386→      .replaceAll(/ð/gi, 'd')
   387→      .replaceAll(/[ÈÉÊË]/gi, 'e')
   388→      .replaceAll(/[ÏÎÍÌ]/gi, 'i')
   389→      .replaceAll(/Ñ/gi, 'n')
   390→      .replaceAll(/[øœÕÔÓÒ]/gi, 'o')
   391→      .replaceAll(/[ÜÛÚÙ]/gi, 'u')
   392→      .replaceAll(/[ŸÝ]/gi, 'y')
   393→      // remove non-chinese, non-latin, non-number, non-space
   394→      .replaceAll(
   395→        /[^\u4E00-\u9FFF\u3040-\u309F\u30A0-\u30FF\uAC00-\uD7AFa-z0-9- ]/gi,
   396→        '',
   397→      )
   398→      .replaceAll(' ', '-')
   399→      .toLowerCase()
   400→  )
   401→}
   402→
   403→const listRule: Partial<
   404→  MarkdownToJSX.Rule<
   405→    MarkdownToJSX.OrderedListNode | MarkdownToJSX.UnorderedListNode
   406→  >
   407→> = {
   408→  render(node, output, state) {
   409→    const Tag = node.ordered ? 'ol' : 'ul'
   410→
   411→    return (
   412→      <Tag
   413→        key={state?.key}
   414→        start={node.type === RuleType.orderedList ? node.start : undefined}
   415→      >
   416→        {node.items.map((item: any, i: number) => {
   417→          let className = ''
   418→          if (item[0]?.type === 'gfmTask') {
   419→            className = 'list-none flex items-center'
   420→          }
   421→
   422→          return (
   423→            <li className={className} key={i}>
   424→              {output(item, state!)}
   425→            </li>
   426→          )
   427→        })}
   428→      </Tag>
   429→    )
   430→  },
   431→}
   432→
```
</details></tool-use>

   113→        return []
   114→    fib = [0, 1]
   115→    for i in range(2, n):
   116→        fib.append(fib[i-1] + fib[i-2])
   117→    return fib[:n]
   118→

   169→## 数学公式 (KaTeX)
   170→
   171→### 行内公式
   172→
   173→爱因斯坦质能方程 $ E = mc^2 $ 是物理学中最著名的公式。
   174→
   175→勾股定理表示为 $ c = \pm\sqrt{a^2 + b^2} $ 。
   176→
   177→二次方程求根公式是 $ x = \frac{-b \pm \sqrt{b^2-4ac}}{2a} $ 。
   178→
   179→也可以不带空格：勾股定理 $c = \pm\sqrt{a^2 + b^2}$ 和多项式 $P(x) = a_nx^n+a_{n-1}x^{n-1} + \dots + a_1x + a_0$ 。
   180→
   181→### 块级公式
   182→
   183→$$
   184→\int_{-\infty}^{\infty} e^{-x^2} dx = \sqrt{\pi}
   185→$$
   186→
   187→$$
   188→P(x) = a_nx^n + a_{n-1}x^{n-1} + \dots + a_1x + a_0
   189→$$
   190→
   191→$$
   192→\sum_{i=1}^{n} i = \frac{n(n+1)}{2}
   193→$$
   194→
   195→$$
   196→\begin{pmatrix}
   197→a & b \\
   198→c & d
   199→\end{pmatrix}
   200→\begin{pmatrix}
   201→x \\
   202→y
   203→\end{pmatrix}
   204→=
   205→\begin{pmatrix}
   206→ax + by \\
   207→cx + dy
   208→\end{pmatrix}
   209→$$

     1→'use client'
     2→
     3→import type { MarkdownToJSX } from 'markdown-to-jsx'
     4→import { Priority } from 'markdown-to-jsx'
     5→import Masonry, { ResponsiveMasonry } from 'react-responsive-masonry'
     6→
     7→import { clsxm } from '~/lib/helper'
     8→import { WrappedElementProvider } from '~/providers/shared/WrappedElementProvider'
     9→
    10→import { Banner } from '../../banner/Banner'
    11→import { Gallery } from '../../gallery/Gallery'
    12→import { Markdown } from '../Markdown'
    13→import { GridMarkdownImage, GridMarkdownImages } from '../renderers/image'
    14→import { pickImagesFromMarkdown } from '../utils/image'
    15→
    16→const shouldCatchContainerName = [
    17→  'gallery',
    18→  'banner',
    19→  'carousel',
    20→
    21→  'warn',
    22→  'error',
    23→  'danger',
    24→  'info',
    25→  'success',
    26→  'warning',
    27→  'note',
    28→
    29→  'grid',
    30→  'masonry',
    31→].join('|')
    32→
    33→export const ContainerRule: MarkdownToJSX.Rule<{
    34→  node: {
    35→    type: string
    36→    params: string
    37→    content: string
    38→  }
    39→}> = {
    40→  match: (source: string) => {
    41→    const result =
    42→      /^\s*::: *(?<type>.*?) *(?:\{(?<params>.*?)\} *)?\n(?<content>[\s\S]+?)\s*::: *(?:\n *)+/.exec(
    43→        source,
    44→      )
    45→
    46→    if (!result) return null
    47→
    48→    const { type } = result.groups!
    49→    if (!type || !type.match(shouldCatchContainerName)) return null
    50→    return result
    51→  },
    52→  order: Priority.MED,
    53→  parse(capture) {
    54→    const { groups } = capture
    55→    return {
    56→      node: { ...groups } as any,
    57→    }
    58→  },
    59→
    60→  render(node, _, state) {
    61→    const { type, params, content } = node.node
    62→
    63→    switch (type) {
    64→      case 'carousel':
    65→      case 'gallery': {
    66→        return (
    67→          <Gallery key={state?.key} images={pickImagesFromMarkdown(content)} />
    68→        )
    69→      }
    70→      case 'warn':
    71→      case 'error':
    72→      case 'danger':
    73→      case 'info':
    74→      case 'note':
    75→      case 'success':
    76→      case 'warning': {
    77→        const transformMap = {
    78→          warning: 'warn',
    79→          danger: 'error',
    80→          note: 'info',
    81→        }
    82→        return (
    83→          <Banner
    84→            type={(transformMap as any)[type] || type}
    85→            className="my-4"
    86→            key={state?.key}
    87→          >
    88→            <WrappedElementProvider className="w-full">
    89→              <Markdown
    90→                value={content}
    91→                allowsScript
    92→                className="w-full [&>p:first-child]:mt-0"
    93→              />
    94→            </WrappedElementProvider>
    95→          </Banner>
    96→        )
    97→      }
    98→      case 'banner': {
    99→        if (!params) {
   100→          break
   101→        }
   102→
   103→        return (
   104→          <Banner
   105→            type={params as 'warn' | 'error' | 'info' | 'success' | 'warning'}
   106→            className="my-4"
   107→            key={state?.key}
   108→          >
   109→            <WrappedElementProvider className="w-full">
   110→              <Markdown
   111→                value={content}
   112→                allowsScript
   113→                className="w-full [&>p:first-child]:mt-0"
   114→              />
   115→            </WrappedElementProvider>
   116→          </Banner>
   117→        )
   118→      }
   119→
   120→      case 'grid': {
   121→        // cols=2,gap=4,rows=2,type=images
   122→
   123→        const { cols, gap = 8, rows, type = 'normal' } = parseParams(params)
   124→
   125→        const Grid: Component = ({ children, className }) => (
   126→          <div
   127→            className={clsxm('relative grid w-full', className)}
   128→            style={{
   129→              gridTemplateColumns: cols
   130→                ? `repeat(${cols}, minmax(0, 1fr))`
   131→                : undefined,
   132→              gap: `${gap}px`,
   133→              gridTemplateRows: rows
   134→                ? `repeat(${rows}, minmax(0, 1fr))`
   135→                : undefined,
   136→            }}
   137→          >
   138→            {children}
   139→          </div>
   140→        )
   141→        switch (type) {
   142→          case 'normal': {
   143→            return (
   144→              <Grid key={state?.key}>
   145→                <Markdown
   146→                  overrides={{
   147→                    img: GridMarkdownImage,
   148→                  }}
   149→                  value={content}
   150→                  allowsScript
   151→                  removeWrapper
   152→                  className="w-full [&>p:first-child]:mt-0"
   153→                />
   154→              </Grid>
   155→            )
   156→          }
   157→          case 'images': {
   158→            const imagesSrc = pickImagesFromMarkdown(content).map((r) => r.url)
   159→
   160→            return (
   161→              <GridMarkdownImages
   162→                height={rows && cols ? +rows / +cols : 1}
   163→                key={state?.key}
   164→                imagesSrc={imagesSrc}
   165→                Wrapper={Grid}
   166→              />
   167→            )
   168→          }
   169→          default: {
   170→            return null
   171→          }
   172→        }
   173→      }
   174→      case 'masonry': {
   175→        const { gap = 8 } = parseParams(params)
   176→        const imagesSrc = pickImagesFromMarkdown(content).map((r) => r.url)
   177→
   178→        return (
   179→          <ResponsiveMasonry
   180→            columnsCountBreakPoints={{
   181→              350: 1,
   182→              750: 2,
   183→            }}
   184→          >
   185→            <Masonry gutter={`${gap}px`} className="[&_figure]:my-0">
   186→              {imagesSrc.map((src) => (
   187→                <GridMarkdownImage key={src} src={src} />
   188→              ))}
   189→            </Masonry>
   190→          </ResponsiveMasonry>
   191→        )
   192→      }
   193→    }
   194→
   195→    return (
   196→      <div key={state?.key}>
   197→        <p>{content}</p>
   198→      </div>
   199→    )
   200→  },
   201→}
   202→
   203→/**
   204→ * gallery container
   205→ *
   206→ * ::: gallery
   207→ * ![name](url)
   208→ * ![name](url)
   209→ * ![name](url)
   210→ * :::
   211→ */
   212→
   213→type ParsedResult = Record<string, string>
   214→
   215→function parseParams(input: string): ParsedResult {
   216→  const regex = /(\w+)=(\w+)/g
   217→  let match: RegExpExecArray | null
   218→  const result: ParsedResult = {}
   219→
   220→  while ((match = regex.exec(input)) !== null) {
   221→    const key = match[1]
   222→    const value = match[2]
   223→    result[key] = value
   224→  }
   225→
   226→  return result
   227→}
   228→
```

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./apps/web/src/components/ui/markdown/parsers/katex.tsx`

```text
     1→'use client'
     2→
     3→import 'katex/dist/katex.min.css'
     4→
     5→import type { MarkdownToJSX } from 'markdown-to-jsx'
     6→import { Priority } from 'markdown-to-jsx'
     7→
     8→import { KateX } from '../../katex'
     9→import { blockRegex, simpleInlineRegex } from '../utils/parser'
    10→
    11→const INLINE_SKIP_R =
    12→  '((?:\\[.*?\\][([].*?[)\\]]|<.*?>(?:.*?<.*?>)?|`.*?`|\\\\\\1|[\\s\\S])+?)'
    13→
    14→//  $ c = \pm\sqrt{a^2 + b^2} $
    15→export const KateXRule: MarkdownToJSX.Rule<{
    16→  katex: string
    17→}> = {
    18→  match: simpleInlineRegex(new RegExp(`^(\\$)(${INLINE_SKIP_R})\\1`)),
    19→  order: Priority.LOW,
    20→  parse(capture) {
    21→    return {
    22→      type: 'kateX',
    23→      katex: capture[2],
    24→    }
    25→  },
    26→  render(node, output, state) {
    27→    return <KateX key={state?.key}>{node.katex}</KateX>
    28→  },
    29→}
    30→export const KateXBlockRule: MarkdownToJSX.Rule<{
    31→  groups: Record<string, string> | undefined
    32→}> = {
    33→  match: blockRegex(
    34→    new RegExp(`^\\s*\\$\\$ *(?<content>[\\s\\S]+?)\\s*\\$\\$ *(?:\n *)+\n?`),
    35→  ),
    36→
    37→  order: Priority.MED,
    38→  parse(capture) {
    39→    return {
    40→      type: 'kateXBlock',
    41→      groups: capture.groups,
    42→    }
    43→  },
    44→  render(node, _, state?) {
    45→    if (!node.groups) {
    46→      return null
    47→    }
    48→    return (
    49→      <div className="scrollbar-none overflow-auto" key={state?.key}>
    50→        <KateX mode="display">{node.groups?.content}</KateX>
    51→      </div>
    52→    )
    53→  },
    54→}
    55→
```
</details></tool-use>

<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./apps/web/src/components/ui/markdown/parsers/mention.tsx`

```text
     1→import type { MarkdownToJSX } from 'markdown-to-jsx'
     2→import { Priority } from 'markdown-to-jsx'
     3→import * as React from 'react'
     4→
     5→import { SocialSourceLink } from '../../rich-link/SocialSourceLink'
     6→import { allowInline } from '../utils/parser'
     7→
     8→// [Innei]{GH@Innei} {TW@Innei} {TG@Innei}
     9→export const MentionRule: MarkdownToJSX.Rule<{
    10→  content: Record<string, string>
    11→}> = {
    12→  match: allowInline((source) => {
    13→    return /^(\[(?<displayName>.*?)\])?\{((?<prefix>(GH)|(TW)|(TG))@(?<name>\w+\b))\}\s?(?!\[.*?\])/.exec(
    14→      source,
    15→    )
    16→  }),
    17→  order: Priority.LOW,
    18→  parse(capture) {
    19→    const { groups } = capture
    20→
    21→    if (!groups) {
    22→      return {
    23→        content: {},
    24→      }
    25→    }
    26→    return {
    27→      content: { ...groups },
    28→    }
    29→  },
    30→  render(result, _, state) {
    31→    const { content } = result
    32→    if (!content) {
    33→      return null as any
    34→    }
    35→
    36→    const { prefix, name, displayName } = content
    37→    if (!name) {
    38→      return null as any
    39→    }
    40→
    41→    return (
    42→      <SocialSourceLink
    43→        name={displayName || name}
    44→        source={prefix}
    45→        key={state?.key}
    46→      />
    47→    )
    48→  },
    49→}
    50→
```
</details></tool-use>

     4→import { Spoiler } from '../renderers/spoiler'

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The web app's `Markdown` component has many browser-specific dependencies (window, document, Next.js Script, FloatPopover, etc.). For a fair benchmark, I should isolate just the core parsing + rendering pipeline of `markdown-to-jsx`.

The key function is `compiler()` from `markdown-to-jsx`. I can call it with the custom rules (KaTeX, container, mention, spoiler, ins) but with simplified renderers that don't use browser APIs.

However, the custom rules' renderers reference many browser-specific components. For a fair benchmark, I should:

1. Use `compiler()` with the custom parser rules (matching/parsing only) but with simple render stubs


2. Include the standard overrides (paragraph, table, heading, code, etc.) with simple React element renderers

For the most realistic comparison, I'll use `compiler()` with the same parser rules, replace browser-specific component renderers with simple stubs, and then use `renderToString` to measure the full parsing, tree building, and React SSR pipeline against the Lexical approach.

Let me set up a separate benchmark file for this markdown-to-jsx comparison.

Actually, I need to reconsider the context here. The web app's Markdown component is client-side in production, but `RichRenderer` is built for SSR. So comparing their `renderToString` performance gives me the SSR comparison I need. For a fair test, I'll run `markdown-to-jsx` `compiler()` with `renderToString` and basic overrides against `RichRenderer` `renderToString` using the `markdown-test` preset, both processing the same content.

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="Write"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Write**</summary>

```tsx
import { readFileSync } from 'node:fs'
import { join, dirname } from 'node:path'
import { fileURLToPath } from 'node:url'
import { createElement } from 'react'
import { renderToString } from 'react-dom/server'

import { RichRenderer } from '../src/index'
import { presets } from '../../rich-editor-demo/src/fixtures/presets'
import { compiler, Priority } from 'markdown-to-jsx'

// ── Config ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

const ITERATIONS = 100
const WARMUP = 10

// ── Stats helpers ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

function median(arr: number[]): number {
  const sorted = [...arr].sort((a, b) => a - b)
  const mid = Math.floor(sorted.length / 2)
  return sorted.length % 2 ? sorted[mid] : (sorted[mid - 1] + sorted[mid]) / 2
}

function p95(arr: number[]): number {
  const sorted = [...arr].sort((a, b) => a - b)
  return sorted[Math.floor(sorted.length * 0.95)]
}

function stats(times: number[]) {
  const avg = times.reduce((s, t) => s + t, 0) / times.length
  const min = Math.min(...times)
  const max = Math.max(...times)
  return { avg, min, max, median: median(times), p95: p95(times) }
}

function fmt(ms: number): string {
  return ms.toFixed(2).padStart(8) + ' ms'
}

function printStats(label: string, times: number[], htmlSize: number) {
  const s = stats(times)
  console.log(`  ${label}`)
  console.log(`    HTML size: ${(htmlSize / 1024).toFixed(1)} KB`)
  console.log(`    avg: ${fmt(s.avg)}  median: ${fmt(s.median)}  p95: ${fmt(s.p95)}`)
  console.log(`    min: ${fmt(s.min)}  max:    ${fmt(s.max)}`)
}

// ── Suppress RichRenderer errors for unknown node types ─────────────────────

const origError = console.error
console.error = (...args: any[]) => {
  if (typeof args[0] === 'string' && args[0].startsWith('[RichRenderer')) return
  origError.apply(console, args)
}

// ── markdown-to-jsx custom rules (ported from web app, render stubs) ────────

const INLINE_SKIP_R = '((?:\\[.*?\\][([].*?[)\\]]|<.*?>(?:.*?<.*?>)?|`.*?`|\\\\\\1|[\\s\\S])+?)'

function simpleInlineRegex(regex: RegExp) {
  const fn: any = (source: string, state: any) => {
    if (state.inline || state.simple) return regex.exec(source)
    return null
  }
  fn.inline = 1
  return fn
}

function blockRegex(regex: RegExp) {
  return (source: string, state: any) => {
    if (state.inline || state.simple) return null
    return regex.exec(source)
  }
}

function allowInline(fn: any) {
  fn.inline = 1
  return fn
}

function parseCaptureInline(capture: any, parse: any, state: any = {}) {
  const isCurrentlyInline = state.inline || false
  const isCurrentlySimple = state.simple || false
  state.inline = true
  state.simple = true
  const result = parse(capture[2], state)
  state.inline = isCurrentlyInline
  state.simple = isCurrentlySimple
  return { children: result }
}

const SpoilerRule = {
  match: simpleInlineRegex(new RegExp(`^(\\|\\|)((?:\\[.*?\\][([].*?[)\\]]|<.*?>(?:.*?<.*?>)?|\`.*?\`|\\\\\\1|\\|\\|.*?\\|\\||[\\s\\S])+?)\\1`)),
  order: Priority.LOW,
  parse: parseCaptureInline,
  render(node: any, output: any, state: any) {
    return createElement('span', { key: state?.key, className: 'spoiler' }, output(node.children, state))
  },
}

const MentionRule = {
  match: allowInline((source: string) => {
    return /^(\[(?<displayName>.*?)\])?\{((?<prefix>(GH)|(TW)|(TG))@(?<name>\w+\b))\}\s?(?!\[.*?\])/.exec(source)
  }),
  order: Priority.LOW,
  parse(capture: any) {
    return { content: { ...(capture.groups || {}) } }
  },
  render(result: any, _: any, state: any) {
    const { prefix, name, displayName } = result.content || {}
    if (!name) return null
    return createElement('span', { key: state?.key, className: 'mention' }, `@${displayName || name}`)
  },
}

const InsertRule = {
  match: simpleInlineRegex(new RegExp(`^(\\+\\+)${INLINE_SKIP_R}\\1`)),
  order: Priority.MED,
  parse: parseCaptureInline,
  render(node: any, output: any, state: any) {
    return createElement('ins', { key: state?.key }, output(node.children, state))
  },
}

const KateXRule = {
  match: simpleInlineRegex(new RegExp(`^(\\$)(${INLINE_SKIP_R})\\1`)),
  order: Priority.LOW,
  parse(capture: any) {
    return { type: 'kateX', katex: capture[2] }
  },
  render(node: any, _: any, state: any) {
    return createElement('span', { key: state?.key, className: 'katex-inline' }, node.katex)
  },
}

const KateXBlockRule = {
  match: blockRegex(new RegExp(`^\\s*\\$\\$ *(?<content>[\\s\\S]+?)\\s*\\$\\$ *(?:\n *)+\n?`)),
  order: Priority.MED,
  parse(capture: any) {
    return { type: 'kateXBlock', groups: capture.groups }
  },
  render(node: any, _: any, state: any) {
    if (!node.groups) return null
    return createElement('div', { key: state?.key, className: 'katex-block' }, node.groups.content)
  },
}

const shouldCatchContainerName = ['gallery', 'banner', 'carousel', 'warn', 'error', 'danger', 'info', 'success', 'warning', 'note', 'grid', 'masonry'].join('|')

const ContainerRule = {
  match: (source: string) => {
    const result = /^\s*::: *(?<type>.*?) *(?:\{(?<params>.*?)\} *)?\n(?<content>[\s\S]+?)\s*::: *(?:\n *)+/.exec(source)
    if (!result) return null
    const { type } = result.groups!
    if (!type || !type.match(shouldCatchContainerName)) return null
    return result
  },
  order: Priority.MED,
  parse(capture: any) {
    return { node: { ...(capture.groups as any) } }
  },
  render(node: any, _: any, state: any) {
    const { type, content } = node.node
    return createElement('div', { key: state?.key, className: `container-${type}` }, content)
  },
}

// ── Compile markdown-to-jsx with matching parser rules ──────────────────────

function compileMarkdown(md: string): any {
  return compiler(md, {
    wrapper: null,
    doNotProcessHtmlElements: ['tab', 'style', 'script'] as any[],
    overrides: {
      video: (props: any) => createElement('video', { ...props, key: props.key }),
      LinkCard: (props: any) => createElement('div', { key: props.key, className: 'link-card' }),
      Gallery: (props: any) => createElement('div', { key: props.key, className: 'gallery' }),
      Tabs: (props: any) => createElement('div', { key: props.key, className: 'tabs' }, props.children),
      tab: (props: any) => createElement('div', { key: props.key, className: 'tab' }, props.children),
      tag: (props: any) => createElement('span', { key: props.key, className: 'tag' }, props.children),
    },
    extendsRules: {
      spoilder: SpoilerRule,
      mention: MentionRule,
      ins: InsertRule,
      kateX: KateXRule,
      kateXBlock: KateXBlockRule,
      container: ContainerRule,
    },
  } as any)
}

function renderMarkdownToString(md: string): string {
  const elements = compileMarkdown(md)
  return renderToString(createElement('div', null, elements))
}

// ── RichRenderer wrapper ────────────────────────────────────────────────────

function renderRichToString(data: any): string {
  return renderToString(
    createElement(RichRenderer, { value: data, variant: 'article', theme: 'light' }),
  )
}

// ── Benchmark runner ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

function bench(label: string, fn: () => string): { times: number[]; htmlSize: number } {
  // warmup
  for (let i = 0; i < WARMUP; i++) fn()

  const times: number[] = []
  let htmlSize = 0
  for (let i = 0; i < ITERATIONS; i++) {
    const start = performance.now()
    const html = fn()
    times.push(performance.now() - start)
    if (i === 0) htmlSize = Buffer.byteLength(html, 'utf-8')
  }
  return { times, htmlSize }
}

// ── Main ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

const __dirname = dirname(fileURLToPath(import.meta.url))
const testingMd = readFileSync(join(__dirname, '../md/testing.md'), 'utf-8')

const markdownTestPreset = presets.find((p) => p.key === 'markdown-test')!

console.log(`Rich Renderer vs markdown-to-jsx Benchmark`)
console.log(`Iterations: ${ITERATIONS} (warmup: ${WARMUP})`)
console.log(`Node: ${process.version}`)
console.log()

// ── Part 1: All presets with RichRenderer ───────────────────────────────────

console.log('═'.repeat(90))
console.log('  PART 1: RichRenderer — all presets')
console.log('═'.repeat(90))

const richAllTimes: number[] = []

for (const preset of presets) {
  const { times, htmlSize } = bench(preset.key, () => renderRichToString(preset.data))
  richAllTimes.push(...times)
  console.log()
  printStats(`${preset.key} (${preset.label})`, times, htmlSize)
}

console.log()
console.log('─'.repeat(90))
console.log('  RichRenderer AGGREGATE')
const richAgg = stats(richAllTimes)
console.log(`    avg: ${fmt(richAgg.avg)}  median: ${fmt(richAgg.median)}  p95: ${fmt(richAgg.p95)}`)
console.log(`    total samples: ${richAllTimes.length}`)
console.log()

// ── Part 2: Head-to-head comparison (markdown-test content) ─────────────────

console.log('═'.repeat(90))
console.log('  PART 2: Head-to-head — same content (markdown-test / testing.md)')
console.log('═'.repeat(90))
console.log()

const richResult = bench('RichRenderer', () => renderRichToString(markdownTestPreset.data))
printStats('RichRenderer (Lexical headless → renderToString)', richResult.times, richResult.htmlSize)

console.log()

const mdResult = bench('markdown-to-jsx', () => renderMarkdownToString(testingMd))
printStats('markdown-to-jsx (compiler → renderToString)', mdResult.times, mdResult.htmlSize)

console.log()
console.log('─'.repeat(90))

const richMed = median(richResult.times)
const mdMed = median(mdResult.times)
const ratio = mdMed / richMed

console.log(`  Comparison (median):`)
console.log(`    RichRenderer:    ${fmt(richMed)}`)
console.log(`    markdown-to-jsx: ${fmt(mdMed)}`)
if (ratio > 1) {
  console.log(`    → RichRenderer is ${ratio.toFixed(1)}x faster`)
} else {
  console.log(`    → markdown-to-jsx is ${(1 / ratio).toFixed(1)}x faster`)
}
console.log()

```

    21→  jsxImportSource: 'react',
    22→  outfile,
    23→  external: [
    24→    'react',
    25→    'react-dom',
    26→    'react-dom/*',
    27→    'react/*',
    28→    'lexical',
    29→    '@lexical/*',
    30→  ],
    31→  banner: {
    32→    js: 'import{createRequire as $$cr}from"module";const require=$$cr(import.meta.url);',
    33→  },
    34→  alias: {
    35→    '@vanilla-extract/css': join(__dirname, 've-stub.mjs'),
    36→  },
    37→  logLevel: 'warning',
    38→})
    39→
    40→try {
    41→  execSync(`node ${outfile}`, { stdio: 'inherit', cwd: root })
    42→} finally {
    43→  try { unlinkSync(outfile) } catch {}
    44→}
    45→
```

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-02-26 15:49:18Z)**_

Benchmark 完成。结果总结：

**Head-to-head 对比（同一内容 testing.md / markdown-test）**

| | HTML | avg | median | p95 |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **RichRenderer** (Lexical headless) | 13.0 KB | 1.45 ms | **1.36 ms** | 1.59 ms |
| **markdown-to-jsx** (compiler) | 18.9 KB | 5.51 ms | **5.01 ms** | 7.64 ms |

**RichRenderer 快 3.7x**（median），且产出 HTML 体积小 31%（13.0 KB vs 18.9 KB）。

注：markdown-to-jsx 侧使用了完整的自定义 parser rules（container/KaTeX/mention/spoiler/ins），renderer 用简化 stubs 替代浏览器依赖组件，确保对等比较公平。实际生产环境中 markdown-to-jsx 带完整 UI 组件会更慢。
